title: Quantization noise and dynamic range in fixed point
description: Each added bit improves the signal-to-quantization-noise ratio by about six decibels, setting the dynamic range a word length can represent.
body: Use when budgeting word lengths for a fixed-point datapath. Do not ignore accumulator growth, since repeated additions extend the needed integer width. Actions: estimate required SNR, derive minimum bits from the six-decibel-per-bit rule, and verify accumulators carry guard bits. Validation signals include a measured quantization SNR matching the bit budget and no accumulator overflow over a long run. Risk boundary: underbudgeting bits buries small signals in quantization noise, so escalate the word length when the noise floor rises into the band of interest.
